publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 86-87. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Sweetest William. This morning [same word repeated, which looks like "snig":] , , , nothing but dreary , which lasted till between 2 & 3 o'clock, when there was a gleam of sun and I managed to get out for a half hour's walk.
I did not sleep well last night, & woke at 7 this morning unable to get to sleep again. I think
I never sleep well when ou are away. Today I have had a little annoyance with my head, but I should say nothing of consequence, & these variations will happen.--

I had an interesting post this morning,-- a curious letter from the Hen about the shilling experiment. I send it to you, but take care of it pray. Also a letter from Dr Locock, expressing the greatest interest & curiosity about the divining rod, & seeming inclined to

join in investigating the subject; in consequence of which I am going to send him in a parcel the vol of containing the accounts of Lady Noel, with Mr Fairholme's second letter, and some remarks & suggestions of my own, together with an account of the shilling experiment.-- I have made up my mind not to let this matter rest, & shall endeavour to turn Faraday's attention to the subject. If he, Mrs Somerville, Babbage, & Dr Locock, would unite

in investigating it, which I think I can manage to effect, probably something would in course of time be made of it.-- Am I not a fine stirrabout meddlesome avis?-- I have so much to write to the Hen, that I must end.--
